title: Remote parity scan event
description: One normalised HTTP request observed by the mock server, tagged with its run identity and classified by category. See tests/remote-parity/README.md.

run_id.description: Identifies the driver run, e.g. 'single-msg-rust-message-count-forward'. Taken from the first path segment of the URL. Must start with an alphanumeric character to avoid collision with reserved underscore-prefixed control endpoints (/_reset, /_log/<run_id>).

scan_round.description: Zero-based round index for 'scan' events. In bidirectional mode a round groups events with distinct roles; encountering a role that is already in the current round opens a new one. Either of the paired forward + backward requests can start a round. Non-scan events use -1.

direction.description: Which cursor the request advances. 'none' for probes, payloads, fallbacks, and errors that are not part of a scan walk.

role.description: Scan-walk role assigned by the classifier. 'fwd_preamble' = 24-byte fetch at a known message_start. 'bwd_postamble' = 24-byte fetch ending at a known message_end. 'bwd_preamble_validation' = reserved for future analysis paths with access to dispatcher state; it is NOT currently emitted because a candidate-preamble validation fetch shares its wire shape with a paired forward preamble and the two cannot be distinguished from observation alone. 'none' for non-scan events.

category.description: 'probe' = HEAD / Range-support check (excluded from parity). 'scan' = a 24-byte preamble or postamble read participating in the current scan walk (compared for parity). 'payload' = bulk data-object fetch or eager-footer chunk fetch (logged, not part of scan parity). 'fallback' = HTTP 200 eager full-body download (logged, not compared). 'error' = non-200/non-206 response (logged, not compared).

logical_range.description: [start, end_exclusive) — the inclusive HTTP byte range 'bytes=a-b' is normalised here to '[a, b+1)'. For HEAD probes with no range, [0, 0].

physical_requests.description: Diagnostic only. NOT compared for parity. The raw HTTP request(s) that produced this logical range. Backends that coalesce multi-range GETs (object_store multipart/byteranges) emit one entry per physical request; backends that issue independent GETs emit one entry.
